---
title: "projet_ML"
author: "Adélie, Yelli, Paulina"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---
## Lecture de notre jeu de données
```{r}
data_project=read.csv("Employee.csv")
```

#Analyse Univariée

## Analyse Statistique
```{r}
colnames(data_project)

dim(data_project)
```

#Target
```{r}
target = factor(data_project$LeaveOrNot, labels = c ("Rester", "Partir"))
data_project$LeaveOrNot = as.factor(data_project$LeaveOrNot)
```

#Analyse de la target
```{r}
summary(data_project$LeaveOrNot)

```

#Répartition
```{r}
quantitatives_vars= data.frame(data_project$JoiningYear, 
                             data_project$PaymentTier,
                             data_project$Age,
                             data_project$ExperienceInCurrentDomain)

qualitatives_vars= data.frame(data_project$Education, data_project$City,
                              data_project$Gender, data_project$EverBenched)
```

#### Les Variables quantitatives
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(quantitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Histogramme de ", colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
  hist(quantitatives_vars[,i], col = i+1, main = main_i, 
       xlab = colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
}

for(i in 1:ncol(quantitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Histogramme de ", colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
  boxplot(quantitatives_vars[,i], col = i+1, main = main_i, 
       xlab = colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i])
}

```
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Barplot de ", colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
  barplot(table(qualitatives_vars[,i]), col = 2:10, main = main_i, 
          xlab = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
}
```

#### Les Variables qualitatives
```{r, out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center"}
for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Barplot de ", colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
  barplot(table(qualitatives_vars[,i]), col = 2:10, main = main_i, 
          xlab = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i])
}
```
Matrice corrélation

```{r cars}
library(reshape2)
library(ggplot2)
quantitatives_vars = data.frame(
  Age = data$Age,
  Experience = data$ExperienceInCurrentDomain,
  JoiningYear = data$JoiningYear
)

# matrice de corrélation
cor_mat = cor(quantitative_vars, use = "complete.obs")
cor_mat

# heatmap
melted_cor_mat = melt(cor_mat)

ggplot(data = melted_cor_mat, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) + 
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Correlation heatmap", x = "", y = "", fill = "corrélation")
```

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
for(i in 1:ncol(quantitatives_vars)){
  main_i = paste0("Boxplot de ", colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i],
                  "\n en fonction du départ de l'entreprise")
  
  boxplot(quantitatives_vars[,i] ~ target,
          col = c("lightblue", "pink"),
          main = main_i,
          ylab = colnames(quantitatives_vars)[i],
          xlab = "LeaveOrNot")
}
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

qualitatives_vars = data.frame(Education = as.factor(data$Education), City = as.factor(data$City), PaymentTier = as.factor(data$PaymentTier), Gender = as.factor(data$Gender), EverBenched = as.factor(data$EverBenched))

for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){

  var_target_counts = melt(table(qualitatives_vars[,i], target))

  xlab_i = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i]

  print(
    ggplot(data = var_target_counts,
           aes(x = Var1, y = value, fill = target)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black") +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(x = xlab_i, y = "Nombre", fill = "LeaveOrNot")
  )
}
```
```{r}
for(i in 1:ncol(qualitatives_vars)){

  var_target_counts = melt(table(qualitatives_vars[,i], target))

  xlab_i = colnames(qualitatives_vars)[i]

  print(
    ggplot(data = var_target_counts,
           aes(x = Var1, y = value, fill = target)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", position = "fill") +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(x = xlab_i, y = "Proportion", fill = "LeaveOrNot")
  )
}
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
